entity.summary: Hong Kong-linked Bitcoin futures and finance-focused exchange formally launched on June 9, 2013. In January 2015, a targeted server compromise and withdrawal-address tampering incident caused a 1,000 BTC loss that shareholders said they would cover; HEI retains inactive status because no definitive final shutdown marker is established.
entity.notes: The launch and 2015 incident are now event-backed. The exchange remains inactive rather than dead because the reviewed evidence does not establish an official shutdown announcement, liquidation, terminal date, or successor transition.

expected.summary: Hong Kong Bitcoin futures and finance-focused exchange operated through 796.com, publicly launched in June 2013 and later associated with a 1,000 BTC withdrawal-address tampering incident in January 2015.
expected.notes: HEI models 796 Exchange as inactive rather than dead because the current evidence set supports historical operation and a major 2015 incident, but does not establish a definitive shutdown announcement or terminal date. Launch date uses the 2013-06-09 public-launch marker referenced from the official Bitcointalk announcement context.

changes.summary: Hong Kong-linked Bitcoin futures and finance-focused exchange formally launched on June 9, 2013. In January 2015, a targeted server compromise and withdrawal-address tampering incident caused a 1,000 BTC loss that shareholders said they would cover; HEI retains inactive status because no definitive final shutdown marker is established.
changes.notes: The launch and 2015 incident are now event-backed. The exchange remains inactive rather than dead because the reviewed evidence does not establish an official shutdown announcement, liquidation, terminal date, or successor transition.

events.title: 796 Exchange formally launched
events.description: 796 Exchange announced the formal opening of its Bitcoin futures and finance-focused trading platform on June 9, 2013.
events.notes: The launch marker is preserved from the public Bitcointalk announcement context.

events.title: 796 Exchange lost 1,000 BTC in a targeted withdrawal incident
events.description: A targeted server compromise and withdrawal-address tampering incident caused 796 Exchange to send 1,000 BTC to an attacker-controlled address; company representatives said major shareholders covered the loss.
events.notes: The article was published January 28 and described the incident as occurring the previous night, so January 27 is used as the event marker.

evidence.title: 796 Exchange formal launch announcement
evidence.notes: Public launch announcement preserving the June 9, 2013 opening marker.

evidence.title: Chinese Exchange Gets 'Goxed' for 1,000 bitcoins
evidence.notes: Contemporary report including comments from a 796 representative on the targeted compromise and shareholder-funded loss coverage.
